title: Silky Light Middle Eastern Cauliflower Bowl
description: A fresh, silky bowl of roasted cauliflower tossed in a creamy tahinilemon sauce, topped with poppy seeds, parsley, and a splash of pomegranate, perfectly paleofriendly.
instructions: Toss cauliflower florets with olive oil, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper until evenly coated.
Spread the cauliflower on a baking sheet in a single layer and roast for 18–20 minutes, turning once, until golden brown and tender.
While the cauliflower roasts, whisk together tahini, lemon juice, a pinch of salt, and a splash of warm water until smooth and silky.
Remove the cauliflower from the oven and let it rest for 2 minutes.
Transfer the cauliflower to a bowl and drizzle the tahini sauce over it, tossing gently to coat.
Sprinkle poppy seeds, chopped parsley, and pomegranate seeds on top.
Serve immediately, optionally with a side of fresh cucumber slices or lettuce leaves.
Enjoy the silky, flavorful crunch of this light Middle Eastern bowl.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
